76354.body:
AI-curated Daily Crypto Digest

Автор: CryptoBeholder.com


Автор: CryptoBeholder.com

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ai-curated-daily-crypto-digest-july-22-cryptobeholder-iawgf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3ByJGYEaZ5TaKoFCtvsk1kiQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3JHRCHTlbeUsk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrwa8miu~2u-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrwa8miu-2u
  
🧠 AI’s Crypto News Brief: Market sentiment is cautious as rising Treasury yields and regulatory hurdles for the CLARITY Act weigh on risk appetite. Despite these headwinds, Robinhood’s surging on-chain activity and massive institutional Bitcoin deals showcase resilient adoption. 📈Robinhood Chain surpasses Base in daily active users less than three weeks after launch Robinhood Chain hit 245,000 daily active users and $9 billion in DEX volume within weeks of launch, overtaking Coinbase's Base. This rapid growth marks a significant retail shift in the Layer 2 ecosystem. Read full story 🏛️SEC’s Peirce warns crypto vaults, onchain lending may fall under securities laws SEC Commissioner Hester Peirce cautioned that decentralized lending products and crypto vaults are not automatically exempt from federal oversight. The warning signals intensified regulatory scrutiny for DeFi yield strategies and custodial services. See details ⚖️Democrats reportedly attempt to block CLARITY Act in Senate: CNBC Senate Democrats are moving to block the CLARITY Act, potentially stalling progress on a comprehensive regulatory framework for digital assets. The political friction comes as analysts lower the probability of Bitcoin reaching $200,000 this year. Read full story 💰Zhibao Technology enters non-binding term sheet with Joyer Tech for $4B Bitcoin PIPE financing Zhibao Technology has announced a massive $4 billion Bitcoin PIPE deal with Joyer Tech to bolster its balance sheet. The move follows a Nasdaq deficiency notice, highlighting a high-stakes pivot into digital asset reserves. See details 🛠️Uniswap’s DualPool hook audited and ready for deployment The DualPool hook for Uniswap v4 is now open-source and audited, allowing liquidity providers to earn vault yield on idle assets. This upgrade aims to significantly improve capital efficiency for decentralized exchange participants. Read full story 📉US Treasury 30-year bond yield exceeds 5% for longest streak since 2007 The 30-year Treasury yield’s prolonged stay above 5% is reshaping risk asset dynamics and increasing debt-servicing costs globally. This persistent macroeconomic pressure continues to challenge the valuation of high-growth tech and crypto sectors. See details 🤖AMD to invest up to $5B in Anthropic, challenging Nvidia in AI infrastructure AMD plans a massive $5 billion investm

76353.subject: Where should you move for higher pay?
76353.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

July 22nd 2026

 

 
 
 
The Economist Today

 Our best journalism, handpicked each day

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDV9AAcAAAAABFCfij3pYkhWqD3OiqXVl0lFtGYJSa_wSym8gL9Nd_lOyzjRMcnbWkZNG6S0x-gPdRiaS_gOL6zT-boQG1THUtqx67AnNJwuARs8Assr647k 


 


 
 

Brooke Unger

Senior digital editor



 Salaries are high in London. It can be tricky to work out why: because skilled people, who would probably earn a lot anywhere, live there? Or because the city itself lifts wages for all? A group of researchers has disentangled the impact of “place” and “person”. Our tool uses the results to compare location premiums across 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDV9AAcAAAAABFCfij3qcGAwttLpPbuSKypn5BjEodVlS90hmYl6LVeGaOt_OaKRtpXmccb7wEJ_UvN_Ye3t2RZc4z1QgWMUNLDVFvTrYhH1LTQlMMc 
hundreds of cities .
 


 Most big coastal countries have a navy and a coastguard. China has a shadowy third force, a “maritime militia”. Lately, it seems, it has been doing something strange and worrying in t

76352.subject: Claude vs Kimi and Qwen: Who wins?
76352.body:
The Atlas

Автор: GenAI Works


Автор: GenAI Works

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/claude-vs-kimi-qwen-who-wins-genai-works-hiwnc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BQGEZE5X5Q6ei8rLicULrHA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3ChlcQHBGWUsk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrw9jir1~r1-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrw9jir1-r1
  
Welcome back! This edition is all about what actually makes agents reliable: memory instead of re-reading, context you can measure, two models arguing their way to a better answer and a skill that makes any assistant get to the point.  In today's Atlas Newsletter: Meterless: an ex-Anthropic engineer open-sources a real memory layer for agents. Qwen3.8-Max vs Kimi K3: two Chinese giants on real code, and why running both won. Agents don't fail alone: a Chicago paper that measures the context behind every failure. i-have-adhd: the GitHub skill that stops your coding assistant from burying the answer. PS: Want to collaborate with the biggest GTM engine and distribution moat for AI companies? You can feature your tool with us and reach millions. An ex-Anthropic engineer just open-sourced a context layer for AI agents It's called Meterless, and it's one of the more ambitious attempts to give AI agents real memory. Most agents today are trapped in a loop.  You give them a task. They complete one step. Then they reread the conversation, reconstruct what happened and reason through the same information again. Every single step. That creates three problems:  → Token costs keep growing → Context windows eventually fill up → The agent still doesn't truly understand your world Meterless takes a different route.  Instead of one enormous prompt, it gives agents several specialized systems:  → H-MEM builds durable, hierarchical memory → The World Model tracks users, tasks, events, documents and relationships → The Markovian Engine breaks long tasks into bounded steps and carries only what the next action needs → Scout detects intent, ambiguity, risk and the right tools before execution begins The compounding effect is the interesting part.  In the project's example, the same 12-step task took 12 chunks without memory and 8 with accumulated memory, for an estimated 814 tokens saved.  A modeled 20-step workflow used 86% fewer input tokens than continuously appending the full history. At 100 steps, the estimate reaches 97%. These figures are modeled rather than independent benchmarks, so treat them as the vendor's for now.  The underlying idea still holds: traditional agent context grows with every step, while Meterless keeps what passes between steps bounded. An agent that forgets everything isn't really an agent. It's a chatbot starting over on repeat. So are you building agent

76347.subject: FIFA Power Rankings: The top performers measured
76347.body:
RANKING FIFA WORLD CUP 2026’S PLAYERS

SEE WHICH STARS TOPPED THE VARIOUS CATEGORIES IN THE FIFA POWER RANKINGS,
POWERED BY ARAMCO

The FIFA Power Rankings provided a new data-led way to measure and showcase
individual player performance throughout the FIFA World Cup 2026™.

Despite emerging as worthy winners, Spain ended up with just a single player
topping one of the categories.

Find out which of the star performers led the way in Attacking, Creativity,
Defending and Goalkeeping.

[All player rankings]
https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/power-rankings?cid=(p_email)(c_fwc)(sc_aramco-p-fwc26)(ssc_fwc-2026)(l_en)(da_220726)(id_fwc26-partnership-aramco-power-rankings-en-220726-email-final)(cc_fwc26)

FIFA POWER RANKINGS WINNERS REVEALED

What did the data tell us about the best attacking, creative and defensive
players at the FIFA World Cup 2026™? Find out who came out on top in those
categories in the FIFA Power Rankings, powered by Aramco.

[Find out more]
https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/final-power-rankings-mbappe-olise-rodri?cid=(p_email)(c_fwc)(sc_aramco-p-fwc26)(ssc_fwc-2026)(l_en)(da_220726)(id_fwc26-partnership-aramco-power-rankings-en-220726-email-final)(cc_fwc26)

THE WORLD CUP’S TOP GOALKEEPERS

Neither of finalists’ No1s topped the two goalkeeping categories. Find out
which keepers came out on top in the FIFA Power Rankings, powered by Aramco.

[Read more]
https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/goalkeepers-final-power-rankings?cid=(p_email)(c_fwc)(sc_aramco-p-fwc26)(ssc_fwc-2026)(l_en)(da_220726)(id_fwc26-partnership-aramco-power-rankings-en-220726-email-final)(cc_fwc26)
